evals.name: single-task-basic
evals.description: Single task execution with Japanese PR language. Tasks directory contains 3 tasks across 2 phases. Task 001010 is a root task with no dependencies.
expected_behavior: Verify clean working tree and on base branch
Check 001010 has no dependencies (root task) — skip dependency validation
Create branch: feature/001010-db-create-users-table
Read task.md and implement according to checklist
Commit at logical boundaries with project convention
Run Task Verify commands
Invoke create-pr skill (PR title and description in Japanese)
Wait for CI with gh pr checks --watch
Merge with --delete-branch
git checkout main && git pull origin main
mv tasks/001010-db-create-users-table tasks/completed/001010-db-create-users-table
Commit and push the completion marker
edge_cases: What if working tree is dirty?
What if already on a feature branch?

evals.name: range-execution-with-dependencies
evals.description: Range execution of 3 tasks in Phase 001. Task 001020 depends on 001010, task 001030 depends on 001020. Tests the full loop including dependency validation between iterations.
expected_behavior: No dependency — execute full cycle (branch, implement, PR, CI, merge, sync, mark complete)
Verify 001010 is in tasks/completed/ — proceed
Execute full cycle
Verify 001020 is in tasks/completed/ — proceed
Execute full cycle
Display completion report: 3 tasks executed, all PR URLs
edge_cases: What if CI fails on task 001020 and can't be fixed?
Does the loop correctly stop and report?
Is the completion marker committed before starting next task?

evals.name: auto-detect-next-task
evals.description: No task specified. Tasks directory has 5 tasks. Tasks 001010 and 001020 are already in completed/. Task 002010 depends on 001010 (satisfied). Task 001030 depends on 001020 (satisfied). Task 002020 depends on 002010 (not satisfied).
expected_behavior: Read dependency-graph.md
Check tasks/completed/ — find 001010 and 001020
Identify ready tasks: 001030 (deps satisfied), 002010 (deps satisfied)
Select lowest sequence in earliest phase: 001030
Execute single task cycle for 001030
edge_cases: What if no tasks are ready (all have unmet dependencies)?
What if completed/ directory doesn't exist yet?
What if multiple tasks in different phases are both ready?

evals.name: ci-failure-retry
evals.description: CI fails after PR creation. Tests the retry logic: read failure details, attempt fix, push, wait again. After 2 failed attempts, stop and notify user.
expected_behavior: Execute Steps 1-5 normally
Step 6: gh pr checks fails
Read failure details with gh pr checks
Attempt to fix the failing check
Push fix commit
Wait for CI again
If fails again: attempt fix #2
If still fails: STOP and output failure report to user
Report includes: which checks failed, what was tried, PR URL
edge_cases: What if the fix introduces new failures?
What if CI is stuck (not failing, just pending)?

evals.name: draft-mode-skip-merge
evals.description: Draft mode: create PRs but skip CI wait and merge. Tests that Steps 6-8 are correctly skipped.
expected_behavior: Execute Steps 1-5 (branch from base, implement, verify, PR as draft)
Skip Step 6 (CI wait and merge)
Step 7: git checkout <base-branch> (no pull — nothing merged)
Skip Step 8 (mark complete — task not merged)
Step 1: Dependency check — 001010 not in completed/ but PR was created in this range → exception applies, proceed
Step 2: Chain branching — create feature/001020 from feature/001010 (not base branch) so 001010 code is available
Execute Steps 3-5 (implement, verify, PR as draft)
Skip Steps 6-8
Display completion report: 2 tasks, both PRs as draft, remind user to review and merge manually
edge_cases: Does chain branching correctly pick up the previous feature branch?
What if the previous feature branch was deleted unexpectedly?
Are deferred completion-marker pushes handled correctly (no push until end)?

evals.name: local-merge-single-task
evals.description: Local merge mode: skip PR creation, merge locally with --no-ff, push to base branch. Verifies that Steps 5 (PR) is skipped, Step 6 uses local merge, Step 7 is no-op, and Step 8 marks complete normally.
expected_behavior: Pre-flight: verify clean tree, on base branch, gh CLI ready
Step 1: Read README.md, validate no dependencies for root task
Step 2: git checkout -b feature/001010-db-create-users-table
Step 3: Implement according to task.md
Step 4: Run Task Verify, task-local tests, full project test suite, lint/typecheck
Step 5: SKIP entirely (--local-merge)
Step 6: git checkout <base> && git pull && git merge --no-ff feature/001010-db-create-users-table && git push && git branch -d
Step 7: Already on base branch, no action needed
Step 8: mv to completed/, commit, push
Completion report: 1 task, local-merge with merge commit SHA, remind no remote CI ran
edge_cases: What if git merge --no-ff reports a conflict?
What if git push is rejected (remote moved)?
What if the base branch has diverged since step 2?

evals.name: dry-run-range
evals.description: Dry run mode on a range: should display the execution plan without performing any mutations. Verifies that no branches are created, no files are modified, and no git write operations occur.
expected_behavior: Pre-flight: verify tasks directory exists, read dependency-graph.md
Resolve range: list all tasks between 001010 and 002030
Display execution plan:
- Task list in order with phase+sequence and full name
- Dependency status for each task (satisfied / pending)
- Mode: normal (no --draft/--skip-ci-wait/--local-merge)
- PR language: ja
- Base branch: auto-detected
No branches created, no files modified, no commits, no PRs
Exit after displaying plan
edge_cases: What if some tasks in the range have unmet dependencies?
Does --dry-run combined with --draft show the correct mode?
What if the tasks directory doesn't exist?

evals.name: new-format-naming-single-task
evals.description: Single task execution using the new 6-3 hyphenated naming format (6-digit PHASE + hyphen + 3-digit SEQUENCE) produced by the updated ywc-task-generator. Verifies that the executor correctly parses the new format and that prefix matching still works with it.
expected_behavior: Parse task specifier: accept hyphenated form 000001-010-db-create-users-table
Pre-flight: verify clean working tree and on base branch
Step 1: Validate dependencies (root task has none)
Step 2: git checkout -b feature/000001-010-db-create-users-table
Step 3: Implement according to task.md
Step 4: Run Task Verify, task-local tests, full suite, lint/typecheck
Step 5-6: Create PR and merge with --delete-branch
Step 7: git checkout <base-branch> && git pull
Step 8: mv tasks/000001-010-db-create-users-table tasks/completed/000001-010-db-create-users-table, commit, push
edge_cases: What if user mixes legacy 001010-* and new 000001-010-* formats in the same session?
Does prefix matching 000001-010 correctly resolve to 000001-010-db-create-users-table?
What if a range spans legacy and new formats (e.g., 001010..000002-010)?
